questions.questionText: The INCORRECT match in the following is :
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
We know, $$\Delta {G^o} = - RT\ln K$$
Case 1 :
If $$\Delta $$Go < 0
$$ \Rightarrow $$ $$- RT\ln K$$ < 0
$$ \Rightarrow $$ $$\ln K$$ > 0
$$ \Rightarrow $$ K > 1

Case 2 :
If $$\Delta $$Go > 0
$$ \Rightarrow $$ $$- RT\ln K$$ > 0
$$ \Rightarrow $$ $$\ln K$$ < 0
$$ \Rightarrow $$ K < 1

Case 2 :
If $$\Delta $$Go = 0
$$ \Rightarrow $$ $$- RT\ln K$$ = 0
$$ \Rightarrow $$ $$\ln K$$ = 0
$$ \Rightarrow $$ K = 1
questions.instruction: Choose the correct option out of the four choices.

questions.questionText:
Two solids dissociate as follows –

The total pressure when both the solids dissociated simultaneously is -
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
Kp1 = P1(P1 + P2)

Kp2 = P2(P1 + P2)

$$ \therefore $$ Kp1 + Kp2 = (P1 + P2)2

$$ \Rightarrow $$ x + y = (P1 + P2)2

$$ \Rightarrow $$ (P1 + P2) = $$\sqrt {x + y} $$ .....(1)

Now total pressure

PT = PC + PB + PE

= (P1 + P2) + P1 + P2

= 2(P1 + P2)

= $$2\sqrt {x + y} $$     [From equation (1)]

questions.questionText:
In a chemical reaction,


the initial concentration of B was 1.5 times of the concentration of A, but the equilibrium concentrations of A and B were found to be equal. The equilibrium constant (K) for the aforesaid chemical reaction is -
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
At equilibrium [A] = [B]

$$ \Rightarrow $$ a - x = 1.5a - 2x

$$ \Rightarrow $$ x = 0.5a

Kc = $${{{{\left[ C \right]}^2}\left[ D \right]} \over {\left[ A \right]{{\left[ B \right]}^2}}}$$

= $${{{{\left( a \right)}^2}\left( {0.5a} \right)} \over {\left( {0.5a} \right){{\left( {0.5a} \right)}^2}}}$$ = 4
questions.instruction: Choose the correct option out of the four choices.

questions.questionText:
For the following reactions, equilibrium constants are given :

S(s) + O2(g) ⇋ SO2(g); K1 = 1052

2S(s) + 3O2(g) ⇋ 2SO3(g); K2 = 10129

The equilibrium constant for the reaction,

2SO2(g) + O2(g) ⇋ 2SO3(g) is :
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
S(s) + O2(g) ⇋ SO2(g); K1 = 1052

By reversing the equation, we get

SO2(g); ⇋ S(s) + O2(g) ; $${1 \over {{K_1}}} = {1 \over {{{10}^{52}}}}$$ ..............(1)

2S(s) + 3O2(g) ⇋ 2SO3(g); K2 = 10129 ....................(2)

Performing (2) - 2 $$ \times $$ (1), we get

2SO2(g) + O2(g) ⇋ 2SO3(g)

$$ \therefore $$ Equilibrium constant of this reaction is

= $${{{K_2}} \over {K_1^2}}$$ = $${{{{10}^{129}}} \over {{{\left( {{{10}^{52}}} \right)}^2}}}$$ = 1025

An open beaker of water in equilibrium with
water vapour is in a sealed container. When a
few grams of glucose are added to the beaker
of water, the rate at which water molecules :
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
With addition of solute in solvent, surface area for vapourisation decreases causes lowering in vapour
pressure.

questions.questionText:
If the equilibrium constant for
A ⇌ B + C is $$K_{eq}^{(1)}$$ and that of
B + C ⇌ P is $$K_{eq}^{(2)}$$, the equilibrium
constant for A ⇌ P is :
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
We have two reactions:

$ A \;\rightleftharpoons\; B + C \quad\text{with } K_{eq}^{(1)}$

$ B + C \;\rightleftharpoons\; P \quad\text{with } K_{eq}^{(2)}$

We want the equilibrium constant for the overall reaction

$ A \;\rightleftharpoons\; P. $

How to combine the equilibrium constants

When two reactions are added to yield a net (overall) reaction, the equilibrium constant of the net reaction is the product of the equilibrium constants of the individual reactions. Formally:

$ \text{(Reaction 1)} + \text{(Reaction 2)} \;\Rightarrow\; \text{(Net Reaction)}, $

and

$ K_{\text{net}} \;=\; K_{\text{(Reaction 1)}} \times K_{\text{(Reaction 2)}}. $

Applying it here

Reaction 1: $A \to B + C$ has $K_{eq}^{(1)}.$

Reaction 2: $B + C \to P$ has $K_{eq}^{(2)}.$

When we add them,

$ \underbrace{A}_{\text{Reactant 1}} \;\longrightarrow\; \underbrace{B + C}_{\text{Products of Reaction 1}=\text{Reactants of Reaction 2}} \;\longrightarrow\; \underbrace{P}_{\text{Product of Reaction 2}}. $

So the net reaction is $A \to P$, and its equilibrium constant is

$ K_{eq}^{\text{(net)}} \;=\; K_{eq}^{(1)} \times K_{eq}^{(2)}. $

Thus, the correct answer is:

$ \boxed{K_{eq}^{(1)} \, K_{eq}^{(2)}}. $

Hence, the answer (Option D) is correct.

rate of the forward (a) and reverse (b) reaction

questions.solution:

rate of forward reaction = Rate of backward reaction

questions.questionText:
For a reaction X + Y ⇌ 2Z , 1.0 mol of X, 1.5 mol
of Y and 0.5 mol of Z were taken in a 1 L vessel and
allowed to react. At equilibrium, the concentration
of Z was 1.0 mol L–1. The equilibrium constant of reaction
is $${x \over {15}}$$. The value of x is _________.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
Keq = $${{{{\left( 1 \right)}^2}} \over {{3 \over 4} \times {5 \over 4}}}$$ = $${{16} \over {15}}$$

$$ \therefore $$ x = 16

questions.questionText:
Consider the following reaction:

N2O4(g) ⇌ 2NO2(g); $$\Delta $$Ho = +58 kJ

For each of the following cases (a, b), the direction in which the equilibrium shifts is :
(a) Temperature is decreased.
(b) Pressure is increased by adding N2 at constant T.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
$$ \because $$ Given reaction is endothermic.

$$ \therefore $$ On decreasing temperature backward reaction will be favoured.

On adding N2, pressure is increased at constant T, and volume would also be constant so no change is observed.
questions.instruction: Choose the correct option out of the four choices.

questions.questionText:
The value of KC is 64 at 800 K for the reaction

N2(g) + 3H2(g) ⇌ 2NH3(g)

The value of KC for the following reaction is :

NH3(g) ⇌ $${1 \over 2}$$N2(g) + $${3 \over 2}$$H2(g)
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
N2(g) + 3H2(g) ⇌ 2NH3(g) ; KC

2NH3(g) ⇌ N2(g) + 3H2(g) ; $${1 \over {{K_C}}}$$

Multiplying by $${1 \over 2}$$, reaction becomes

NH3(g) ⇌ $${1 \over 2}$$N2(g) + $${3 \over 2}$$H2(g) ;

$$ \therefore $$ New KC = $${\left( {{1 \over {{K_C}}}} \right)^{{1 \over 2}}}$$ = $${\left( {{1 \over {64}}} \right)^{{1 \over 2}}}$$ = $${1 \over 8}$$
questions.instruction: Choose the correct option out of the four choices.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
To determine the correct relationship between the equilibrium constants $K_C$ and $K_p$ for the reaction:

$$\text{Fe}_2\text{N(s)} + \frac{3}{2}\text{H}_2\text{(g)} \rightleftharpoons 2\text{Fe(s)} + \text{NH}_3\text{(g)}$$

First, consider the general relationship between $K_C$ and $K_p$:

$$ K_p = K_C(RT)^{\Delta n} $$

where $\Delta n$ is the change in the number of moles of gas between the products and the reactants, $R$ is the ideal gas constant, and $T$ is the temperature in Kelvin.

For the given reaction, calculate $\Delta n$:

Moles of gas in products: NH3(g) = 1 mole

Moles of gas in reactants: $${3 \over 2}$$ H2(g) = 1.5 moles

Therefore:

$$ \Delta n = 1 - 1.5 = -0.5 $$

Substitute this value into the equation relating $K_C$ and $K_p$:

$$ K_p = K_C(RT)^{-0.5} $$

Rearrange to express $K_C$:

$$ K_C = K_p(RT)^{0.5} $$

Thus, the correct option is:

Option A: $$K_C = K_p(RT)^{1/2}$$

questions.questionText:
The variation of equilibrium constant with temperature is given below :

Temperature Equilibrium Constant
T1 = 25oC K1 = 10
T2 = 100oC K2 = 100

The values of $$\Delta $$Ho, $$\Delta $$Go at
T1 and $$\Delta $$Go at T2 (in kJ mol–1) respectively, are close to :
[Use R = 8.314 J K–1 mol–1]
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
ln $$\left[ {{{{k_2}} \over {{k_1}}}} \right]$$ = $${{\Delta H^\circ } \over R}\left\{ {{1 \over {{T_1}}} - {1 \over {{T_2}}}} \right\}$$

$$ \Rightarrow $$ ln(10) = $${{\Delta H^\circ } \over R}\left\{ {{1 \over {298}} - {1 \over {373}}} \right\}$$

$$ \Rightarrow $$ $${\Delta H^\circ }$$ = 28.37 kJ/mol

$$\Delta $$Go = –RT ln K

T1 = 25oC K1 = 10

$$\Delta $$Go at T1 = –8.314 × 298 × 2.303 × log 10

= –5.71 kJ/mol

$$\Delta $$Go at T2 = –8.314 × 373 × 2.303 × log(100)

= –14.29 kJ/mol

questions.questionText: In the figure shown below reactant A (represented by square) is in equilibrium with product B (represented by circle). The equilibrium constant is :
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
In the figure, Reactant A is represented by square.
And there are 6 squares. So at equilibrium
[A]eq = 6

In the figure, Product B is represented by circle.
And there are 11 circles. So at equilibrium
[B]eq = 11

For reaction, A ⇌ B

Keq = $${{\left[ B \right]} \over {\left[ A \right]}}$$ = $${{11} \over 6}$$ $$ \approx $$ 2

If we start the reaction in a closed container at 495 K with 22 millimoles of A, the amount of B in the equilibrium mixture is ____________ millimoles.
(Round off to the Nearest Integer). [R = 8.314 J mol$$-$$1 K$$-$$1; ln 10 = 2.303]
